# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-05-31

Public AI endpoints are economically attractive to abuse; per-request verification is becoming table-stakes.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Protecting against token theft
   Public AI endpoints are economically attractive to abuse; per-request verification is becoming table-stakes.

2. How we contain Claude across products
   If you run agents with tool access, treat containment as an environment problem first; UI prompts alone are not a control.

3. The Website Specification
   Useful as an audit baseline and as a canonical ‘agent-readiness’ target for docs/sites you ship.

4. Microsoft Office 2019 and 2021 for Mac view-only conversion
   If you (or clients) rely on Office 2019 on older Macs, plan a migration before July 13, 2026.

5. Running Python ASGI apps in the browser via Pyodide + a service worker
   Viable shape for shipping ‘serverless’ demos/tools (FastAPI, Datasette) as static hosting with real app behavior.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-05-31

Draft status: not approved for posting.
